draft.introduction: Jesus’ great invitation: “Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.” The passage uses wisdom language, presenting Jesus as divine Wisdom incarnate offering rest to the weary.

translation.text: Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.
translation.original_text: δεῦτε πρός με πάντες οἱ κοπιῶντες καὶ πεφορτισμένοι κἀγὼ ἀναπαύσω ὑμᾶς

translator_notes.note: “Rest for your souls” (ἀνάπαυσιν ταῖς ψυχαῖς ὑμῶν) quotes Jeremiah 6:16 LXX. The “yoke” metaphor was standard rabbinic language for Torah obligation. Jesus offers his own yoke as a replacement—a staggering claim to authority equal to Torah itself.
